methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros, a seed-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Daniel Kultran and Grant Verstandig, has raised $30M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by reference to a broader frontier-tech climate universe spanning nine private firms and one public equity (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $75M valuation. Peer-median analysis anchors to the three comparable companies, which cluster around a $150M median with a range of $110M–$200M, reflecting variability in technology maturity and capital efficiency across the cohort. The peer approach directly reflects market pricing for analogous-stage competitors in the same sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $64M–$172M, with a median of $112M. The stage-progression method yields the lower bound, while the peer set anchors the upper range. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three peer comparables (limited sample size in frontier-climate equity), relative technology differentiation versus peers, and path-to-revenue timing. The median estimate of $112M reflects reasonable equidistance between methodologies but should be treated as order-of-magnitude guidance pending deeper operational due diligence.
